concepts.name: Linked List
concepts.short_definition: A linear data structure where elements are not stored at contiguous memory locations.

concepts.name: Node
concepts.short_definition: The basic building block of a linked list, containing data and a pointer to the next node.

concepts.name: Head Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A pointer that points to the first node of a linked list.

concepts.name: Tail Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A pointer that points to the last node of a linked list.

concepts.name: Next Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A pointer within a node that refers to the subsequent node in the list.

concepts.name: Singly Linked List
concepts.short_definition: A linked list where each node points only to the next node.

concepts.name: Doubly Linked List
concepts.short_definition: A linked list where each node points to both the next and previous nodes.

concepts.name: Circular Linked List
concepts.short_definition: A linked list where the last node points back to the first node.

concepts.name: Sorted Linked List
concepts.short_definition: A linked list where the elements are arranged in a specific order (ascending or descending).

concepts.name: Unsorted Linked List
concepts.short_definition: A linked list where the elements are not arranged in any particular order.

concepts.name: Merge Operation
concepts.short_definition: The process of combining two or more linked lists into a single linked list.

concepts.name: Merging Sorted Lists
concepts.short_definition: Combining two sorted linked lists into a single sorted linked list.

concepts.name: Iterative Merging
concepts.short_definition: Merging linked lists using loops and explicit pointer manipulation.

concepts.name: Recursive Merging
concepts.short_definition: Merging linked lists by breaking down the problem into smaller, self-similar subproblems.

concepts.name: Pointer Manipulation
concepts.short_definition: The act of changing the 'next' or 'previous' pointers of nodes to restructure the list.

concepts.name: Temporary Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A pointer used to hold a reference to a node during list manipulation, preventing data loss.

concepts.name: Dummy Node
concepts.short_definition: A placeholder node often used at the beginning of a merged list to simplify edge cases.

concepts.name: In-place Merge
concepts.short_definition: Merging linked lists without allocating significant extra memory, by rearranging existing nodes.

concepts.name: Time Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of how the execution time of an algorithm grows with the input size.

concepts.name: Space Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of how the memory usage of an algorithm grows with the input size.

concepts.name: Comparison
concepts.short_definition: The act of comparing elements from two lists to determine their order in the merged list.

concepts.name: Iteration
concepts.short_definition: Traversing through the nodes of a linked list sequentially.

concepts.name: Recursion
concepts.short_definition: A programming technique where a function calls itself to solve a problem.

concepts.name: Base Case
concepts.short_definition: The condition in a recursive function that stops the recursion.

concepts.name: Recursive Step
concepts.short_definition: The part of a recursive function that breaks down the problem and calls itself.

concepts.name: Edge Cases
concepts.short_definition: Unusual or extreme conditions that need to be handled in an algorithm.

concepts.name: Empty List
concepts.short_definition: A linked list with no nodes.

concepts.name: Single Node List
concepts.short_definition: A linked list containing only one node.

concepts.name: Merging Empty Lists
concepts.short_definition: Handling the scenario where one or both lists to be merged are empty.

concepts.name: Merging Single Node Lists
concepts.short_definition: Handling the scenario where one or both lists to be merged have only one node.

concepts.name: List Traversal
concepts.short_definition: Visiting each node in a linked list.

concepts.name: Node Assignment
concepts.short_definition: Assigning a node to a pointer variable.

concepts.name: Pointer Comparison
concepts.short_definition: Comparing the values pointed to by two pointers.

concepts.name: Tail Append
concepts.short_definition: Adding a node to the end of a linked list.

concepts.name: Head Insertion
concepts.short_definition: Adding a node to the beginning of a linked list.

concepts.name: Interleaving Lists
concepts.short_definition: Merging lists by alternating nodes from each list.

concepts.name: Merge Sort (Linked List)
concepts.short_definition: A sorting algorithm that uses merging of linked lists.

concepts.name: Auxiliary Space
concepts.short_definition: Extra memory used by an algorithm beyond the input storage.

concepts.name: Constant Space
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm that uses a fixed amount of extra memory, regardless of input size.

concepts.name: Linear Space
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm whose extra memory usage grows linearly with the input size.
